Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes.
(Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Marked lines in parentheses when a behavior was observed only once.)

A. Identification and initial data retrieval
- IF a user requests order-related actions → THEN require identification: either (name + zip) OR email before any order action.
- IF user provides name+zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) before further actions.
- IF user provides email → THEN use the email to locate the user (Observed Behavior: in trajectories only name+zip was used; still require an identifier).

B. Order discovery and inspection
- IF user-level details obtained → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for every order referenced in the user's orders list when needed to determine status/items.
- IF asked "how many options are available" for a product → THEN call list_all_product_types() to get product_id, then call get_product_details(product_id) and count variants where "available": true; report that count (trajectory: T-Shirt count = 10).
- IF you need to filter items to act on (e.g., "pending small t-shirt orders") → THEN inspect get_order_details for each order and select only items with order.status == "pending" and item.options matching requested constraints (e.g., size "S").

C. Determining allowed operation type by order status
- IF order.status == "pending" → THEN you may modify items (modify_pending_order_items) and modify the order's shipping address (modify_pending_order_address).
- IF order.status == "delivered" → THEN you must use return_delivered_order_items to return or exchange_delivered_order_items to exchange delivered items; do not use modify_pending_order_* tools.
- IF order.status == "cancelled" → THEN do not attempt to modify/return/exchange (no trajectory showing changing cancelled orders).

D. Address changes
- IF user requests address correction for pending orders AND account → THEN:
  - call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, new_address) for each pending order the user asked to update,
  - call modify_user_address(user_id, new_address) to update the account address,
  - report success after tool outputs confirm updated address (trajectory: modified two pending orders and the user account).

E. Item modifications for pending orders
- IF user wants to change items in a pending order → THEN:
  - Confirm the target pending order(s) and the specific item_ids to change,
  - Call get_product_details(product_id) to find matching/new variants (filter by user constraints),
  - Choose new_item_id(s) according to user constraints (see rule F for selection logic),
  - Call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[old_item_ids], new_item_ids=[new_item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id),
  - Provide summary of modified order and any price difference shown by the tool output.
- IF a pending-order modification will change the order price → THEN ask/confirm which payment method to use (present available payment methods from get_user_details). Use that payment_method_id in modify_pending_order_items.

F. Selection logic for replacement variants (applies to modify_pending_order_items and exchange_delivered_order_items)
- IF user specifies "keep all other features the same" → THEN filter get_product_details(product_id).variants to those whose options exactly match each specified feature (e.g., resolution == 24MP and storage == SD card). Only consider variants that match all specified attributes.
- IF user asks for "the cheapest version" AND explicitly states they don't care about other options → THEN select the variant with available == true and the lowest price among all variants (ignore other differing options).
- IF user gives partial constraints (e.g., wants same material but not color) → THEN filter only on the explicitly stated constraints and then apply cheapest/other selection among the filtered set.

G. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Identify original item_ids to exchange,
  - Use get_product_details to find acceptable replacement variants following selection logic (rule F),
  - Confirm with user that exchange is irreversible until accepted,
  - Ask which payment method they want to use for any price difference (present options from get_user_details),
  - Call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[old_item_ids], new_item_ids=[new_item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id).
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - Confirm refund destination/payment method (default to original payment method only after user confirmation),
  - Warn the user that the return/exchange operation is one-time/irreversible for this order and require explicit user confirmation,
  - Call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id).

H. Mutual exclusivity constraints (observed)
- IF items to be returned and items to be exchanged are in the same order → THEN inform the user they cannot both return and exchange items within the same order in a single operation; require user to choose either return or exchange for that order (trajectory: Conversation 1).
- IF user requests multiple return/exchange operations on the same order that would violate the "one-time" limitation → THEN warn user and require explicit confirmation, and proceed only after they confirm.

I. Confirmation and irreversible operation handling
- IF an operation is irreversible / cannot be changed until accepted (returned/exchanged) → THEN explicitly state this to the user and request explicit confirmation before calling modify/return/exchange tools (trajectories: confirmations before returns/exchanges/one-time warnings).
- IF user confirms → THEN proceed with the appropriate tool call.

J. Price calculations and comparisons
- IF assessing savings, price difference, or refund totals → THEN call calculate(expression="...") to compute sums/differences and present numeric results (rounded to two decimals as provided by tools).
- IF comparing exchange vs return savings → THEN fetch variant prices via get_product_details and compute difference(s) via calculate before proposing options (trajectory: calculated savings for pet bed and chair and total).
- IF modifying a pending item results in a price change → THEN the modify_pending_order_items tool may show small extra payments or refunds; report those results to user.

K. Payment method handling
- IF a price difference or additional charge arises from a modification/exchange → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use and include the chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refunds to a specific payment method (e.g., PayPal) → THEN use that payment method id when calling return_delivered_order_items (trajectory: used paypal_2433177).
- IF user does not specify payment method for pending-order modification → THEN confirm "use original payment method?" and proceed only when user agrees (trajectories: asked and user confirmed).

L. Availability and user choices
- IF a desired variant is available == false → THEN inform the user it is unavailable and present alternatives (trajectories: product details show available flags; assistant asked follow-up options).
- IF user accepts a variant with a price/resolution/storage difference from original → THEN proceed following exchange rules and confirm payment method when needed.

M. Refund timing and speed (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- Observed: assistant told user refund processing takes 5–7 business days and that the agent cannot speed up bank processing. Mark as "Observed Behavior (not proven mandatory)". If user requests faster processing, respond that you cannot speed up standard banking processing (trajectory: Conversation 4).

N. Counting available variants
- IF user asks "how many of X are available" → THEN return the count of variants with available == true from get_product_details(product_id) (trajectory: counted 10 available T-Shirt variants).

O. Post-action confirmation & closing
- AFTER any successful modify/return/exchange tool call → THEN present a short summary of what was done and ask "Anything else?" (observed consistently).
- IF user replies "No" → THEN close the session politely.

P. Tool usage order (operational sequence to follow)
- Identification → find_user_id_by_name_zip or lookup by email
- User record retrieval → get_user_details(user_id)
- Order inspection → get_order_details(order_id) for involved orders
- Product discovery (if needed) → list_all_product_types() → get_product_details(product_id)
- Price math → calculate(expression)
- Confirm irreversible actions & payment method
- Execute change → modify_pending_order_items or modify_pending_order_address or modify_user_address or return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items
- Report results and ask if further help

Q. Evidence flags
- Rules marked above but labeled "(Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)" are based on single-occurrence behaviors in trajectories (refund processing time).
- All other rules are directly supported by multiple trajectory examples (tool sequences, confirmations, constraints, selection logic).

End of policy.

Updated Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Mark lines "(Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)" where a behavior was observed only once or not proven mandatory.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- IF a user requests any order-related action → THEN require identification: either (first_name + last_name + zip) OR email before any order action.
- IF user provides name+zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) before further actions.
- IF user provides email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email) → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) before further actions.

B. User record & available controls
- IF get_user_details(user_id) returns payment_methods → THEN use that map to present or select payment_method_id for price differences or refunds.
- IF get_user_details(user_id) returns orders → THEN inspect those order IDs with get_order_details when the user references or asks to act on their orders.

C. Order inspection and status checks
- IF you need to decide actionability for an order → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) and use order.status to decide allowed operations.
- IF order.status == "pending" → THEN allowed operations: modify items (modify_pending_order_items) and modify shipping address (modify_pending_order_address) for that order.
- IF order.status == "delivered" → THEN disallow modify_pending_order_* tools; use return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items for returns/exchanges.
- IF order.status == "cancelled" → THEN do not attempt modify/return/exchange operations on that order.
- IF order.status != "pending" (e.g., "processed") → THEN do not call modify_pending_order_items or modify_pending_order_address; inform user the order cannot be modified (trajectory: processed orders could not be changed).
  - (This is a concrete prohibition based on observed trajectories: assistant treated "processed" as not modifiable.)

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- IF user requests account address change → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
- IF user requests updating address "for my pending orders" or "for all orders" → THEN:
  - Call get_order_details for each order in user's orders list,
  - For each order where order.status == "pending" AND the user requested updating that order → call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip).
  - After modify_user_address and all modify_pending_order_address calls succeed → report success and which orders were updated.
- IF user requests to change address for non-pending orders (processed/delivered) → THEN inform user those orders cannot be updated.

E. Cancelling pending orders
- IF user asks to cancel pending order(s) → THEN:
  - Confirm which pending order_ids to cancel,
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory) ask for a reason for cancellation/return if the assistant requests it,
  - Call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each confirmed pending order,
  - After each cancel_pending_order returns, report the cancellation and any refund recorded in the order's payment_history.
- IF multiple pending orders are cancelled → THEN you may call cancel_pending_order repeatedly for each order and sum refunds if the user asks for total refunded.

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- IF user wants to change items in a pending order → THEN:
  - Confirm the target pending order_id(s) and the exact old item_id(s) to change,
  - Call list_all_product_types() if you need to find product_id for a product name (or use product_id from get_order_details if present),
  - Call get_product_details(product_id) to fetch variants,
  - Apply selection logic (Rule G) to choose replacement variant item_id(s),
  - IF the modification will change price → THEN present available payment_methods from get_user_details and ask which payment method to use; obtain explicit user choice (or explicit confirmation to "use original payment method"),
  - Call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[old_item_ids], new_item_ids=[new_item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id),
  - After tool returns, present a short summary showing changed items and any price difference or refund as reported by the tool.
- IF user requests modifying multiple pending orders → THEN repeat above for each pending order the user confirmed.

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- IF user specifies "keep all other features the same" (explicit constraints) → THEN filter get_product_details(product_id).variants to those whose options exactly match each specified feature; only consider variants matching all specified attributes.
- IF user asks for "the cheapest version" AND explicitly says they don't care about other options → THEN select among variants with available == true the variant with the lowest price.
- IF user gives partial constraints (e.g., only material) → THEN filter variants only on the explicitly stated constraints, then apply cheapest/other selection among filtered set.
- IF chosen variant available == false → THEN inform user it's unavailable and present alternatives; do not call modify/ exchange with an unavailable variant.

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the original delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Use get_product_details(product_id) and selection logic (Rule G) to choose replacement variant item_id(s),
  - Explicitly state the exchange is irreversible until accepted and ask for explicit confirmation from the user,
  - Ask which payment method they want to use for any price difference, present payment options from get_user_details,
  - Call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[old_item_ids], new_item_ids=[new_item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id).
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (if user wants original payment method, confirm explicitly),
  - Explicitly warn the user the return/exchange operation is one-time/irreversible for that order and require explicit user confirmation,
  - Call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id).

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- IF items to be returned and items to be exchanged are in the same order → THEN inform the user they cannot both return and exchange items within the same order in a single operation; require the user to choose one action for that order (trajectory evidence).
- IF a return/exchange operation or a modification would violate the observed "one-time" limitation on that order → THEN warn the user about the one-time limitation and proceed only after explicit confirmation.
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory): assistants warned "I can only modify orders once" before modifying pending orders in at least one trajectory. Apply same confirmation practice when modifying pending orders if such warning is appropriate.

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- IF an operation is irreversible or recorded as "return requested" / "pending (item modified)" etc. → THEN explicitly state this to the user and request explicit user confirmation before calling modify_pending_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, or exchange_delivered_order_items.
- IF user explicitly confirms → THEN proceed with the appropriate tool call.

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method (e.g., PayPal) → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items.
- IF user does not specify a payment method for a pending-order modification → THEN ask "Use original payment method?" and proceed only after user confirms.
- AFTER modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items returns → THEN report any refunds or additional payment recorded in the operation (as shown by tool output).

L. Price math and calculations
- IF assessing savings, price differences, or refund totals → THEN call calculate(expression="...") to compute sums or differences and present numeric results rounded to two decimals (as provided by tools).
- IF comparing exchange vs return savings → THEN fetch variant prices via get_product_details and compute difference(s) via calculate before making a recommendation.

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- IF user asks "how many X are available" → THEN call list_all_product_types() → get_product_details(product_id for X) → count variants where variant.available == true → report that count (trajectory: T-Shirt count = 10).
- IF list_all_product_items tool is not found → THEN use list_all_product_types() as observed fallback. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- Identification → find_user_id_by_name_zip OR find_user_id_by_email
- User record retrieval → get_user_details(user_id)
- Order inspection → get_order_details(order_id) for involved orders
- Product discovery (if needed) → list_all_product_types() → get_product_details(product_id)
- Price math → calculate(expression)
- Confirm irreversible actions & payment method with user
- Execute change → modify_pending_order_items OR modify_pending_order_address OR modify_user_address OR cancel_pending_order OR return_delivered_order_items OR exchange_delivered_order_items
- Report results and ask "Anything else?"

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF desired variant.available == false → THEN inform the user it is unavailable and present alternatives from get_product_details (filter by explicit user constraints).
- IF user accepts a variant that differs in price/options → THEN proceed following exchange/modification rules and confirm payment method when needed.

P. Refund timing and bank speed (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- Observed in a trajectory: assistant told user refunds take 5–7 business days and that the agent cannot speed up bank processing. If user asks, respond that you cannot speed up standard banking processing. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- AFTER any successful modify/return/exchange/cancel/modify_user_address tool call → THEN present a short summary of what was done and ask "Anything else?".
- IF user replies "No" → THEN close the session politely.

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- Rules marked "(Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)" are based on single-occurrence or uncertain evidence in the trajectories (e.g., asking for cancellation reasons; fallback to list_all_product_types when list_all_product_items tool missing; warning that pending modifications are one-time).
- All other rules are supported by multiple trajectories (tool sequences, confirmations, constraints, selection logic).

End of policy.

Updated Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "(Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)" denote behaviors observed only once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- IF a user requests any order-related action → THEN require identification before any order action: either (first_name + last_name + zip) OR email.
- IF user provides name+zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) before further actions.
- IF user provides email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email) → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) before further actions.

B. User record & available controls
- IF get_user_details(user_id) returns payment_methods → THEN use that map to present or select payment_method_id for price differences or refunds.
- IF get_user_details(user_id) returns orders → THEN inspect those order IDs with get_order_details(order_id) whenever the user references or asks to act on their orders (or when you need to identify addresses on file).
- IF the user refers to a "saved profile address" or "another address on file" → THEN examine get_user_details.address and also scan addresses in get_order_details for the user's orders and present any distinct addresses found.
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory): assistants scanned other orders to locate alternate addresses and presented them to the user.

C. Order inspection and status checks
- IF you need to decide actionability for an order → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) and use order.status to decide allowed operations.
- IF order.status == "pending" → THEN allowed operations on that order: modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, cancel_pending_order.
- IF order.status == "delivered" → THEN disallow modify_pending_order_* tools; use return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items for returns/exchanges.
- IF order.status == "cancelled" → THEN do not attempt modify/return/exchange/cancel operations on that order.
- IF order.status != "pending" (e.g., "processed") → THEN do not call modify_pending_order_items or modify_pending_order_address; inform user the order cannot be modified.

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- IF user requests account address change → THEN check get_user_details.address:
  - IF get_user_details.address already equals the requested new address → THEN do NOT call modify_user_address; inform the user that their account address is already set to that address and ask "Anything else?"
    - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory): seen when assistant detected no change needed and skipped modify_user_address.
  - ELSE call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). After success, report success and ask "Anything else?"
- IF user requests updating address "for my pending orders" or "for all orders" → THEN:
  - Call get_order_details for each order in user's orders list,
  - For each order where order.status == "pending" AND the user explicitly requested updating that order → call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip),
  - After modify_user_address (if requested) and all modify_pending_order_address calls succeed → report success and list which orders were updated.
- IF user requests to change address for non-pending orders (processed/delivered) → THEN inform user those orders cannot be updated.

E. Cancelling pending orders
- IF user asks to cancel pending order(s) → THEN:
  - Call get_order_details for all user orders to identify which orders have status == "pending",
  - Confirm with the user which pending order_id(s) to cancel,
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory) ask for a reason for cancellation and include that reason in the cancel_pending_order call if provided,
  - Call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each confirmed pending order,
  - After each cancel_pending_order returns, report each cancellation and any refund recorded in the returned order.payment_history.
- IF multiple pending orders are cancelled → THEN you may call cancel_pending_order repeatedly for each order and sum refunds if the user asks for a total refunded.

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- IF user wants to change items in a pending order → THEN:
  - Confirm the target pending order_id(s) and the exact old item_id(s) to change,
  - Call list_all_product_types() if you need to find product_id for a product name (or use product_id from get_order_details if present),
  - Call get_product_details(product_id) to fetch variants and their availability/prices,
  - Apply selection logic (Rule G) to choose replacement variant item_id(s),
  - IF the modification will change price → THEN present available payment_methods from get_user_details and ask which payment method to use; obtain explicit user choice (or explicit confirmation to "use original payment method"),
  - IF user asks to "use the same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and confirm with the user before using it,
  - Warn the user if applicable about one-time modification limits before proceeding (see I). Ask explicit confirmation for irreversible changes (see J).
  - Call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[old_item_ids], new_item_ids=[new_item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id),
  - After tool returns, present a short summary showing changed items and any price difference or refund as reported by the tool and shown in order.payment_history.
- IF user requests modifying multiple pending orders → THEN repeat above for each pending order the user confirmed.

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- IF user specifies "keep all other features the same" (explicit constraints) → THEN filter get_product_details(product_id).variants to those whose options exactly match each specified feature; only consider variants matching all specified attributes.
- IF user asks for "the cheapest version" AND explicitly says they don't care about other options → THEN select among variants with available == true the variant with the lowest price.
- IF user gives partial constraints (e.g., only material) → THEN filter variants only on the explicitly stated constraints, then apply cheapest/other selection among filtered set.
- IF chosen variant available == false → THEN inform user it's unavailable and present alternatives; do not call modify/exchange with an unavailable variant.
- IF an exact-match request cannot be satisfied → THEN inform the user which explicit constraint(s) prevented a match and offer alternatives (e.g., relax one attribute or change color/size). Proceed only after user selects or confirms an alternative.
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory): assistant explicitly told user "That option doesn't exist" and offered alternatives before proceeding.

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Use get_product_details(product_id) and selection logic (Rule G) to choose replacement variant item_id(s),
  - Explicitly state the exchange is irreversible until accepted and ask for explicit confirmation from the user,
  - Ask which payment method they want to use for any price difference, present payment options from get_user_details (or confirm "use original payment method"),
  - Call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[old_item_ids], new_item_ids=[new_item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id),
  - After tool returns, present the operation result, including exchange_price_difference (positive or negative) and any payment/refund recorded in order.payment_history.
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (if user wants original payment method, confirm explicitly),
  - Explicitly warn the user the return/exchange operation is one-time/irreversible for that order and require explicit user confirmation,
  - Call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id),
  - After tool returns, report the return status and any refund recorded in the returned order object.

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- IF items to be returned and items to be exchanged are in the same order → THEN inform the user they cannot both return and exchange items within the same order in a single operation; require the user to choose one action for that order.
  - This was observed across trajectories and must be enforced when present.
- IF a return/exchange operation or a modification would trigger a one-time limit on that order → THEN warn the user about the one-time limitation and require explicit confirmation before proceeding.
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory): assistants explicitly warned "I can only modify orders once" before some pending-order modifications.

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- IF an operation is irreversible or will be recorded as "return requested", "exchange requested", or "pending (item modified)" → THEN explicitly state this to the user and request explicit user confirmation before calling modify_pending_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, or exchange_delivered_order_items.
- IF user explicitly confirms → THEN proceed with the appropriate tool call.

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method (e.g., PayPal) → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF user does not specify a payment method for a pending-order modification → THEN ask "Use original payment method?" and proceed only after user confirms.
- AFTER modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items returns → THEN report any refunds or additional payment recorded in the operation (as shown by tool output).

L. Price math and calculations
- IF assessing savings, price differences, or refund totals → THEN call calculate(expression="...") to compute sums or differences and present numeric results rounded to two decimals.
- IF comparing exchange vs return savings → THEN fetch variant prices via get_product_details and compute difference(s) via calculate before making a recommendation.

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- IF user asks "how many X are available" → THEN call list_all_product_types() → get_product_details(product_id for X) → count variants where variant.available == true → report that count.
- IF list_all_product_items tool is not found → THEN use list_all_product_types() as observed fallback.
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- Identification → find_user_id_by_name_zip OR find_user_id_by_email
- User record retrieval → get_user_details(user_id)
- Order inspection → get_order_details(order_id) for involved orders (and scan all orders if user asks e.g., "cancel all pending" or to identify addresses)
- Product discovery (if needed) → list_all_product_types() → get_product_details(product_id)
- Price math → calculate(expression)
- Confirm irreversible actions & payment method with user
- Execute change → modify_pending_order_items OR modify_pending_order_address OR modify_user_address OR cancel_pending_order OR return_delivered_order_items OR exchange_delivered_order_items
- Report results and ask "Anything else?"

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF desired variant.available == false → THEN inform the user it is unavailable and present alternatives from get_product_details (filter by explicit user constraints).
- IF user accepts a variant that differs in price/options → THEN proceed following exchange/modification rules and confirm payment method when needed.

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests or if there is no alternative payment method on file.
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory): assistant offered transfer to human when gift card balance was insufficient and user had no other payment method.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the standard processing cannot be sped up by the agent (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory). If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline (e.g., "5–7 business days") only as Observed Behavior.

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- AFTER any successful modify/return/exchange/cancel/modify_user_address tool call → THEN present a short summary of what was done and ask "Anything else?".
- IF user replies "No" → THEN close the session politely.

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- Rules marked "(Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)" are based on single-occurrence or uncertain evidence in the trajectories (e.g., scanning other orders to find saved addresses; offering human transfer on insufficient balance; skipping modify_user_address when address already matches).
- All other rules are supported by multiple trajectories (tool sequences, confirmations, constraints, selection logic).

End of policy.

- IF user requests to change address for non-pending orders (processed/delivered/cancelled) → THEN inform user those orders cannot be updated.

E. Cancelling pending orders
- IF user asks to cancel pending order(s) → THEN:
  - Call get_order_details for all user orders to identify which orders have status == "pending",
  - Confirm with the user which pending order_id(s) to cancel,
  - Inform the user that cancel_pending_order cancels the entire order; cancelling a single item from a pending order is not supported. (Supported by trajectory evidence)
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory) ask for a reason for cancellation and include that reason in the cancel_pending_order call if provided,
  - Call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each confirmed pending order,
  - After each cancel_pending_order returns, report each cancellation and any refund recorded in the returned order.payment_history.
- IF multiple pending orders are cancelled → THEN you may call cancel_pending_order repeatedly for each order and sum refunds if the user asks for a total refunded.

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- IF user wants to change items in a pending order → THEN:
  - Confirm the target pending order_id(s) and the exact old item_id(s) to change,
  - Call list_all_product_types() if you need to find product_id for a product name (or use product_id from get_order_details if present),
  - Call get_product_details(product_id) to fetch variants and their availability/prices,
  - Apply variant selection logic (Rule G) to choose replacement variant item_id(s),
  - IF the modification will change price → THEN present available payment_methods from get_user_details and ask which payment method to use; obtain explicit user choice (or explicit confirmation to "use original payment method"),
  - IF user asks to "use the same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and confirm with the user before using it,
  - Warn the user if applicable about one-time modification limits before proceeding (see I). Ask explicit confirmation for irreversible changes (see J).
  - Call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[old_item_ids], new_item_ids=[new_item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id),
  - After tool returns, present a short summary showing changed items and any price difference or refund as reported by the tool and shown in order.payment_history.
- IF user requests modifying multiple pending orders → THEN repeat above for each pending order the user confirmed.
- IF user requests only to update one of several changes previously planned (e.g., change items but not the address) → THEN proceed only for the confirmed subset of changes.

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- IF user specifies "keep all other features the same" (explicit constraints) → THEN filter get_product_details(product_id).variants to those whose options exactly match each specified feature; only consider variants matching all specified attributes.
- IF user asks for "the cheapest version" AND explicitly says they don't care about other options → THEN select among variants with available == true the variant with the lowest price.
- IF user gives partial constraints (e.g., only material) → THEN filter variants only on the explicitly stated constraints, then apply cheapest/other selection among filtered set.
- IF chosen variant available == false → THEN inform user it's unavailable and present alternatives; do not call modify/exchange with an unavailable variant.
- IF an exact-match request cannot be satisfied → THEN inform the user which explicit constraint(s) prevented a match and offer alternatives (e.g., relax one attribute or change color/size). Proceed only after user selects or confirms an alternative.
- IF the user requests a replacement that is a different product type than the original (e.g., luggage → coat) → THEN inform user exchanges can only be within the same product type/product_id and do NOT call modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items.
- IF user requests "same item/variant replacement" (i.e., new_item_id equals old_item_id) to get a fresh replacement → THEN it is allowed; call exchange_delivered_order_items with the same item_id(s) as new_item_ids if user explicitly requests that. (Supported by trajectory evidence)

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Use get_product_details(product_id) and selection logic (Rule G) to choose replacement variant item_id(s),
  - Explicitly state the exchange is irreversible until accepted and ask for explicit confirmation from the user,
  - Ask which payment method they want to use for any price difference, present payment options from get_user_details (or confirm "use original payment method"),
  - IF the exchange will require the user to pay additional money → THEN require explicit payment_method selection for that payment (cannot proceed without a chosen payment method).
  - Call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[old_item_ids], new_item_ids=[new_item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id),
  - After tool returns, present the operation result, including exchange_price_difference (positive or negative) and any payment/refund recorded in order.payment_history.
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. (Supported by trajectory evidence)
  - Explicitly warn the user the return/exchange operation is one-time/irreversible for that order and require explicit user confirmation,
  - Call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids=[item_ids], payment_method_id=chosen_payment_method_id) — but only accept payment_method_id that corresponds to the original payment method on the order (see previous rule),
  - After tool returns, report the return status and any refund recorded in the returned order object.

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- IF items to be returned and items to be exchanged are in the same order → THEN inform the user they cannot both return and exchange items within the same order in a single operation; require the user to choose one action for that order.
- IF a return/exchange operation or a modification would trigger a one-time limit on that order → THEN warn the user about the one-time limitation and require explicit confirmation before proceeding.
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory): assistants explicitly warned "I can only modify orders once" before some pending-order modifications.

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- IF an operation is irreversible or will be recorded as "return requested", "exchange requested", or "pending (item modified)" → THEN explicitly state this to the user and request explicit user confirmation before calling modify_pending_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, or exchange_delivered_order_items.
- IF user explicitly confirms → THEN proceed with the appropriate tool call.
- IF user gives a clear affirmative (e.g., "Go ahead", "Yes, proceed", or "Yes, I'm sure") → treat as explicit confirmation.

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. (Supported by trajectory evidence)
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF user does not specify a payment method for a pending-order modification that will change price → THEN ask "Use original payment method?" and proceed only after user confirms.
- AFTER modify_pending_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items or return_delivered_order_items returns → THEN report any refunds or additional payment recorded in the operation (as shown by tool output).

L. Price math and calculations
- IF assessing savings, price differences, or refund totals → THEN call calculate(expression="...") to compute sums or differences and present numeric results rounded to two decimals.
- IF comparing exchange vs return savings → THEN fetch variant prices via get_product_details and compute difference(s) via calculate before making a recommendation.

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- IF user asks "how many X are available" → THEN call list_all_product_types() → get_product_details(product_id for X) → count variants where variant.available == true → report that count.
- IF list_all_product_items tool is not found → THEN use list_all_product_types() as observed fallback.
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- Identification → find_user_id_by_name_zip OR find_user_id_by_email
- User record retrieval → get_user_details(user_id)
- Order inspection → get_order_details(order_id) for involved orders (and scan all orders if user asks e.g., "cancel all pending" or to identify addresses)
- Product discovery (if needed) → list_all_product_types() → get_product_details(product_id)
- Price math → calculate(expression)
- Confirm irreversible actions & payment method with user
- Execute change → modify_pending_order_items OR modify_pending_order_address OR modify_user_address OR cancel_pending_order OR return_delivered_order_items OR exchange_delivered_order_items
- Report results and ask "Anything else?"

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF desired variant.available == false → THEN inform the user it is unavailable and present alternatives from get_product_details (filter by explicit user constraints).
- IF user accepts a variant that differs in price/options → THEN proceed following exchange/modification rules and confirm payment method when needed.
- IF multiple orders contain the same product type or name → THEN ask the user to confirm which order_id they want to act on (present order addresses and statuses to disambiguate).

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests or if there is no alternative payment method on file.
  - (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory): assistant offered transfer to human when gift card balance was insufficient and user had no other payment method.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory). If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- AFTER any successful modify/return/exchange/cancel/modify_user_address tool call → THEN present a short summary of what was done (items changed, orders updated, payment/refund amounts) and ask "Anything else?".
- IF user replies "No" → THEN close the session politely.

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- Rules marked "(Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)" are based on single-occurrence or uncertain evidence in the trajectories (e.g., scanning other orders to find saved addresses; trying an alternate email after a failed find_user_id_by_email; offering human transfer on insufficient balance; skipping modify_user_address when address already matches).
- All other rules are supported by multiple trajectories (tool sequences, confirmations, constraints, selection logic).

End of policy.
